Name: quality_robustness_under_clinical_shift
Title: Does quality-aware preprocessing improve robustness under clinical image shift?
Experiment: Compare identity, grayscale, autocontrast, equalization, and auto-equalization preprocessing under safe augmentation policies for dental radiograph classification. Test whether radiograph-aware preprocessing improves the benchmark primary metric and reduces sensitivity to clinically realistic image-quality variation.

Name: calibration_and_thresholds_for_clinical_objectives
Title: Can calibrated decision rules outperform better feature extractors for clinical dental classification?
Experiment: Compare weighted cross-entropy, focal loss, and balanced softmax on the imbalanced dental classification benchmark, then test whether validation-based threshold selection and simple calibration analysis improve clinically relevant operating points such as recall, specificity, and calibration error more than backbone changes alone.
